properties.name: Metema → Gallabat
properties.temporal_note: Updated: see C-ET-SD-001 for full temporal flow data

properties.name: Gallabat → Metema (Sudan Conflict Exodus)
properties.data_source: IOM DTM Ethiopia Flow Monitoring Dashboard

temporal_flows.notes: First 10 days after Sudan conflict. 1000+/day peak. Conflict started Apr 15.
temporal_flows.source: IOM DTM Ethiopia April 2023 Flow Monitoring Dashboard

temporal_flows.notes: Metema dominated inflows. Sudan inflows exceeded outflows — rare reversal.
temporal_flows.source: IOM DTM Ethiopia May 2023 + ReliefWeb DREF MDRET030

temporal_flows.notes: Inflows still dominant — ceasefire talks not holding.
temporal_flows.source: IOM DTM Ethiopia June 2023

temporal_flows.notes: All 6 Ethiopia FMPs. Flow reverting toward historic outflow pattern.
temporal_flows.source: IOM DTM Ethiopia Flow Monitoring April 2024

temporal_flows.notes: Stable mixed flow. 1,164 avg/day.
temporal_flows.source: IOM DTM Ethiopia Flow Monitoring May 2024

temporal_flows.notes: 2,942 forced movements June 17-July 7 at Metema specifically.
temporal_flows.source: IOM DTM Ethiopia Sudan Movements Update 1 Jan–7 Jul 2024

temporal_flows.notes: Peak month 2024. 1,377 avg/day.
temporal_flows.source: IOM DTM Ethiopia Flow Monitoring July 2024

temporal_flows.notes: 11% decrease from July.
temporal_flows.source: IOM DTM Ethiopia Flow Monitoring August 2024

temporal_flows.notes: METEMA CLOSED Sep 2 — insecurity on Ethiopian side. 23.5% drop.
temporal_flows.source: IOM DTM Ethiopia Flow Monitoring September 2024

temporal_flows.notes: Metema REOPENED Oct 20. Partial month data.
temporal_flows.source: IOM DTM Ethiopia Flow Monitoring October 2024

temporal_flows.notes: Stabilising post-closure. 1,004 avg/day.
temporal_flows.source: IOM DTM Ethiopia Flow Monitoring November 2024

temporal_flows.notes: 11.8% increase. Year-end surge.
temporal_flows.source: IOM DTM Ethiopia Flow Monitoring December 2024

temporal_flows.notes: 1.9% decrease. 1,102 avg/day.
temporal_flows.source: IOM DTM Ethiopia Flow Monitoring January 2025

temporal_flows.notes: PARTIAL CLOSURE — Gallabat-Metema CBP partially inaccessible Dec 2025 due to security tensions.
temporal_flows.source: IOM DTM Sudan Cross-Border Monitoring Report Feb 2026

cumulative_since_conflict.note: 3.3M total cross-border from Sudan since April 2023 conflict (IOM end 2024)

properties.name: Kampala → Nimule → Juba (Lifeline Corridor)
properties.context: Busiest land border in South Sudan. Primary supply route for Juba. Transit corridor for Sudan returnees.
properties.data_source: IOM South Sudan DTM Flow Monitoring Registry + IOM Border Management Programme

temporal_flows.notes: IOM confirmed: 8,000+ people/month. Established baseline pre-OSBP.
temporal_flows.source: IOM South Sudan border management training report July 2017

temporal_flows.notes: OSBP Nimule side officially opened. Formalised crossing infrastructure.
temporal_flows.source: Trademark East Africa / IOM South Sudan

temporal_flows.notes: Renk County: 10,735 individuals (8,130 returnees + 2,605 others) — intention survey by DTM.
temporal_flows.source: IOM DTM South Sudan Renk intention survey July 7 2024

temporal_flows.notes: Upper Nile: 39,736 displaced in Renk County alone. Sudan conflict pressure reaching SS via this corridor.
temporal_flows.source: IOM DTM South Sudan Aug 16-31 2024

temporal_flows.notes: Oct 1-15: flood displacement. Aweil West 7,775 + Unity State 1,954 + Abyei 990.
temporal_flows.source: IOM DTM South Sudan October 2024

temporal_flows.notes: September: 7,481 returnees arrived in Juba, Fangak, Fashoda, Wau — all from Sudan crisis.
temporal_flows.source: IOM DTM South Sudan September 2024

temporal_flows.notes: Renk County VAS conducted. Ongoing return flows documented.
temporal_flows.source: IOM DTM South Sudan Village Assessment Survey Renk County May 2025

infrastructure.uganda_side: Elegu OSBP — completed Nov 2018, financed by Trademark East Africa $5M
infrastructure.road_juba_nimule: 119 miles / Highway A43 — paved with international assistance
infrastructure.road_gulu_nimule: 65 miles / Highway A104 — murram surface degrades in rain

properties.name: Dolo Ado → Doolow (Horn Mixed Migration)
properties.context: Active IOM Flow Monitoring. Highest incoming movements in Somalia FMP network. Bidirectional: Somali refugees to Ethiopia, economic migrants to Somalia/onward.
properties.data_source: IOM Somalia DTM Border Point Flow Monitoring 2024-2025

temporal_flows.notes: Total movements all 7 Somalia FMPs. 13% increase vs 2023. 75% outgoing 25% incoming.
temporal_flows.source: IOM Somalia DTM Annual Flow Monitoring 2024

temporal_flows.notes: Doolow: -6% vs May. Highest incoming FMP alongside Dhobley 28%.
temporal_flows.source: IOM DTM Somalia June 2024

temporal_flows.notes: Oct 2024 movements across 7 Somalia FMPs. Doolow consistently highest incoming.
temporal_flows.source: IOM DTM Somalia October 2024

temporal_flows.notes: 23% decrease from Dec 2024. FM paused Jan 2025. Doolow -13%.
temporal_flows.source: IOM DTM Somalia February 2025

temporal_flows.notes: 23% increase vs Aug 2025.
temporal_flows.source: IOM DTM Somalia September 2025

temporal_flows.notes: 2% decrease vs Sep 2025. Doolow +8%.
temporal_flows.source: IOM DTM Somalia October 2025

properties.name: Kananga → Kazumba → Dundo (Kasai/Angola Ring)
properties.context: Ring displacement corridor. DRC Kasai conflict drives families to Angola. Angola economic deterioration drives return. Ring continues. 186,776 Angola returnees documented in Kazumba territoire alone.
properties.data_source: IOM/OCHA DRC Kasai-Central displacement assessment (your uploaded file)

temporal_flows.notes: Kasai conflict peak. Mass flight to Angola. Up to 400,000 DRC nationals entered Angola.
temporal_flows.source: UNHCR DRC displacement reports 2016-2018

temporal_flows.notes: Return wave from Angola. Kazumba: 186,776 Angola returnees. Ring closing westward.
temporal_flows.source: IOM/OCHA Kasai-Central APERCU dataset

temporal_flows.notes: Simultaneous: DRC returnees 511K + PDIs 377K + Angola returnees 325K. Triple displacement event.
temporal_flows.source: IOM/OCHA APERCU Kasai-Central (uploaded dataset)

temporal_flows.notes: Ring continues. Tshibala-Kalomba-Ndekesha axis remains highest pressure zone.
temporal_flows.source: Ongoing IOM DRC monitoring

properties.name: Kosti → Renk → Malakal (Sudan Crisis Corridor)
properties.context: Primary South Sudan-Sudan border corridor. White Nile route. 39,736 displaced to Renk County in 2 weeks Aug 2024. Sudan returnees reconstitute South Sudanese IDP caseload.
properties.data_source: IOM DTM South Sudan + IOM DTM Sudan Cross-Border Monitoring

temporal_flows.notes: Sudan conflict onset. SS-Sudan border activates as return corridor for South Sudanese who fled to Sudan.
temporal_flows.source: IOM Sudan conflict tracking April 2023

temporal_flows.notes: 39,736 displaced to Renk County alone in 15 days. CRITICAL surge. Majority previously in Sudan.
temporal_flows.source: IOM DTM South Sudan Aug 16-31 2024

temporal_flows.notes: DTM rapid intention survey: 8,130 returnees + 2,605 others in Renk TC1/TC2/Extension.
temporal_flows.source: IOM DTM South Sudan Renk intention survey 7 July 2024

temporal_flows.notes: 7,481 returnees in Juba+Fangak+Fashoda+Wau. All from ongoing Sudan crisis.
temporal_flows.source: IOM DTM South Sudan September 2024

temporal_flows.notes: 3.3M total cross-border from Sudan to all 6 neighbouring countries. SS among destinations.
temporal_flows.source: IOM Sudan End of Year Report 2024
